definition: I2S excels at solving upstream structural CMP roadblocks (length, CRC, magic), inflating the corpus with large deeply-decoded valid inputs; the target bytes receive no random mutation because the corpus is dominated by structurally-complex images rather than short inputs that probe the target condition.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S clears structural CMP roadblocks (length, CRC, magic) and drives the corpus toward large valid images deep in IDAT decoding, starving bKGD chunk bytes of random mutation; naive keeps inputs small and blindly randomizes chunk bytes, eventually flipping a high background byte non-zero.
signature.one_line: Non-zero high background bytes; I2S drags corpus to valid IDAT-heavy inputs

analysis.summary_one_line: A short PNG whose color-type bKGD chunk carries a non-zero high byte in buf[0], buf[2] or buf[4]

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A truecolor (RGB) bKGD chunk reached with bit_depth<=8 and at least one of the high-order background bytes buf[0], buf[2] or buf[4] non-zero, so that the line-2018 disjunction takes its TRUE side ('invalid color').
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive produces short inputs (375-793 bytes) that error out of image decoding early and re-enter the bKGD path repeatedly while havoc randomizes the chunk payload bytes; it reaches L2018 with T=13 F=1, i.e. it lucky-hits a non-zero buf[0]/buf[2]/buf[4] many times.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog produces large, structurally-valid inputs (8360-8771 bytes) that pass the length/CRC roadblocks and sink execution into IDAT row decoding; at L2018 it is stuck at T=0 F=19, always seeing all-zero background bytes and never flipping a high byte non-zero.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: This is an I2S-anti branch where the I2S fuzzer LOSES. cmplog's comparison-solving substitution clears the structural integer-CMP roadblocks (length, CRC, magic) and drives the corpus toward big valid images deep in IDAT decoding, starving the bKGD color bytes of random mutation. naive's plain havoc byte/word mutation, having no CMP introspection to chase, keeps inputs small and blindly randomizes the chunk bytes, so it lucky-flips a high background byte non-zero. The winning technique is havoc-only mutation, not any CMP-solving.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive (winner) over cmplog (loser) on the I2S technique delta.
The blocked TRUE side requires buf[0], buf[2] or buf[4] to be non-zero.
The TRUE (blocked) side is the globally blocked side at this branch.
naive resolves the branch nearly always while cmplog never resolves it.
cmplog is blocked in all ten trials.
On the call chain, naive takes the TRUE side many times (T=13) while cmplog never does (T=0).
cmplog (L) spends thousands of invocations in IDAT row-decoding functions that naive (W) never enters.
Winner naive seeds are small (e.g. 375 bytes).
Loser cmplog seeds are large (e.g. 8759 bytes).
naive's winning mutation_op chains are pure havoc byte/word mutators with no I2S.
naive has no I2S mutator; its mutation set is havoc + token only.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes logged CMP operands at matching input offsets, which solves structural roadblocks.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with a non-zero-color gate behind a bKGD-style chunk in which cmplog resolves the gate as fast as or faster than naive, i.e. I2S substitution reaching the color bytes rather than only the structural roadblocks.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff section shows only diffuse PARTIAL differences in the IHDR/gAMA/sRGB header region, not at the actual bKGD color bytes, so the precise dataflow offset of the winning non-zero byte is inferred from execution-depth and file-size divergence rather than read directly off the seed bytes.
